visits.note: Patient presented with symptoms of fatigue and increased thirst. Blood work revealed elevated HbA1c (7.2) and BP (140/90). Diagnoses of Type 2 Diabetes and Hypertension were made. Medications were initiated for HbA1c control (Metformin 500mg BID) and BP control (Lisinopril 10mg QD).

Patient reported worsening fatigue and decreased mobility. Blood work revealed increased HbA1c (8.1) and decreased eGFR (60). Medications were adjusted to include Statin (Atorvastatin 20mg QD) for lipid management and ACE inhibitor (Enalapril 5mg BID) for kidney protection. Diagnoses of Chronic Kidney Disease were made.

Patient reported increased shortness of breath and chest pain. Blood work revealed increased HbA1c (9.0) and decreased eGFR (45). Coronary Artery Disease was diagnosed due to the presence of chest pain. Medications were adjusted to include Beta blocker (Metoprolol 50mg BID) and Nitrate (Isosorbide mononitrate 30mg BID) for cardiovascular management.
